heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: On 02 September 2025, the rain fell at an exceptional rate, peaking at 34.3mm per hour, the heaviest downpour recorded.  A total of 21.19mm fell over the 15.0 hour storm.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 23, which occurred on 07 September 2025 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: A significant dry period is on-going and is ranked 7th for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 9 consecutive days beginning 23 December 2025.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 6 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 26.7°C, observed on 29 April 2025 when the temperature fell to 0.4°C but also managed to reach 27.1°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 6 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (198°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 8.2°C), outside temperature (down 5.0°C), and feels like temperature (down 4.2°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 286 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 8.8°C), outside temperature (up 9.0°C), cloud levels (up 324 m), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 286.0m lower than yesterday, 324.3m lower than the past week, 32.4m higher than the past month.

Yesterday’s cloud levels showed a low of 828m and a peak of 2280m, averaging 994.9m; the week and month recorded warmer averages.
cloud levels.summary: Partial cloudiness with some periods of sun.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; bitterly cold temperatures are expected. Also, the air will feel dry and calm and still conditions are expected. Showers, partial cloudiness with some periods of sun, and bright intervals likely. Stable conditions are expected under a high pressure system.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-5.9°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
